name: The Slag Galleries
description: This side gallery was carved not by water or fire but by something that clawed the living rock away in long parallel grooves, leaving walls ribbed like the inside of a throat. Rivulets of hardened slag hang frozen down them in grey-black ropes, and the floor is a treacherous crust of the same, thin enough in places to show a sullen red glow beneath. The heat here is close and stifling, and the ribbed walls throw back every sound doubled, so that a single dripping seems to come from all around at once.
ambient_messages: A rope of hardened slag creaks and sheds a rain of grit that patters across the crusted floor.
Somewhere in the ribbed dark a single heavy drop falls, and the walls throw the sound back from every side at once.
The crust underfoot ticks and shifts, and a seam of red glow briefly widens before the slag settles shut again.
